description: Machine-checkable inventory for Rust helper modules shipped from std/rust/native/*.rs. Each entry declares the owning typed facade, classification, runtime contract, allowed code dependencies, forbidden growth, evidence owner, and review line budget.

entries.whyNotLoweringToday: The facade needs generated-crate callback aliases and hxrt array slice helpers that are not yet expressible as a clean Haxe extern-only signature.
forbiddenGrowth: portable Array API expansion beyond scoped borrow callbacks
entries.evidenceOwner: test/snapshot/rust_array_slice_views and scripts/ci/check-metal-policy.sh slice-view output-shape case

entries.whyNotLoweringToday: The helper centralizes a typed Clone bound until generic method lowering can emit this operation directly from the Haxe facade.
entries.evidenceOwner: rust.RefTools scoped-borrow fixtures and native facade manifest guard

entries.whyNotLoweringToday: The helper exposes a small typed facade over std::time::Duration construction, inspection, and sleep before these pure calls are represented as compiler intrinsics.
entries.evidenceOwner: rust.DurationTools snapshots and native facade manifest guard

entries.whyNotLoweringToday: The helper exposes typed std::collections::HashMap operations while bridging owned iterator results through the current hxrt iterator representation.
forbiddenGrowth: clone-heavy adapters beyond explicit owned iterator conversions
entries.evidenceOwner: test/snapshot/rust_hashmap and native facade manifest guard

entries.whyNotLoweringToday: The nullable-string variant mirrors hash_map_tools.rs while returning hxrt::string::HxString where the portable string contract requires it.
forbiddenGrowth: string compatibility behavior outside the nullable-string contract
entries.evidenceOwner: test/snapshot/rust_hashmap nullable-string coverage and native facade manifest guard

entries.whyNotLoweringToday: The helper keeps std::time::Instant operations behind a typed facade until simple native value method lowering covers this surface.
entries.evidenceOwner: rust.InstantTools snapshots and native facade manifest guard

entries.whyNotLoweringToday: The helper exposes Vec::into_iter through a typed facade until iterator construction can lower directly.
entries.evidenceOwner: rust.IterTools snapshots and native facade manifest guard

entries.whyNotLoweringToday: The helper centralizes generated-crate map storage access for Haxe map overrides, including HxRef borrowing and owned iterator bridge results.
forbiddenGrowth: portable map semantics outside owned storage operations
entries.evidenceOwner: map storage snapshots and native facade manifest guard

entries.whyNotLoweringToday: The nullable-string variant centralizes generated-crate map storage access when Haxe String lowers to hxrt::string::HxString.
forbiddenGrowth: string compatibility behavior outside the nullable-string contract
entries.evidenceOwner: map storage nullable-string snapshots and native facade manifest guard

entries.whyNotLoweringToday: The helper exposes a small typed facade over mutable Rust slices until slice operations lower directly from the borrow-region facade.
entries.evidenceOwner: test/snapshot/rust_array_slice_views, borrow-region negative fixtures, and native facade manifest guard

entries.whyNotLoweringToday: The helper owns direct std::fs file/path operations for the Rust-native metal facade without portable sys.io stream handles.
forbiddenGrowth: platform abstraction beyond the facade contract
entries.evidenceOwner: test/positive/metal_no_hxrt_native_file and scripts/ci/check-metal-policy.sh native-file output-shape case

entries.whyNotLoweringToday: The helper owns process lifecycle, cwd/env setup, stdin partial-move behavior, child wait/kill semantics, and byte/UTF-8 output validation behind typed process facades.
forbiddenGrowth: stream runtime unrelated to CommandChild lifecycle
entries.evidenceOwner: test/positive/metal_no_hxrt_command_* fixtures and scripts/ci/check-metal-policy.sh native-process/command-child output-shape cases

entries.whyNotLoweringToday: SocketAddr.localhost/localhostDetailed/port are compiler-lowered. The retained helper exists only because current Haxe externs cannot declare the private wrapper field and crate-private std::net conversions used by TCP/UDP helpers without a helper or native-wrapper generation support.
entries.evidenceOwner: test/positive/metal_no_hxrt_socket_addr and scripts/ci/check-metal-policy.sh socket-address output-shape case

entries.whyNotLoweringToday: The helper provides typed socket error categories shared by TCP/UDP helpers without exceptions, Dynamic payloads, or a broad error runtime.
entries.evidenceOwner: test/positive/metal_no_hxrt_socket_errors, test/positive/metal_no_hxrt_socket_addr, and native facade manifest guard

entries.whyNotLoweringToday: The helper owns direct std::net::TcpListener/TcpStream handles, blocking read/write/shutdown lifecycle, address binding, and byte validation behind typed TCP facades.
entries.evidenceOwner: test/positive/metal_no_hxrt_native_tcp, test/positive/metal_no_hxrt_tcp_bytes, test/positive/metal_no_hxrt_socket_addr, and scripts/ci/check-metal-policy.sh TCP output-shape cases

entries.whyNotLoweringToday: The helper owns direct std::net::UdpSocket handles, address binding, datagram send/receive behavior, and byte validation behind typed UDP facades.
entries.evidenceOwner: test/positive/metal_no_hxrt_native_udp, test/positive/metal_no_hxrt_udp_bytes, test/positive/metal_no_hxrt_socket_addr, and scripts/ci/check-metal-policy.sh UDP output-shape cases

entries.whyNotLoweringToday: The helper exposes typed std::ffi::OsString conversions until OS-string value conversion lowering is represented directly.
forbiddenGrowth: portable string compatibility beyond explicit lossy conversion
entries.evidenceOwner: OS string snapshots and native facade manifest guard

entries.whyNotLoweringToday: The nullable-string variant mirrors os_string_tools.rs while bridging to hxrt::string::HxString for the portable string contract.
forbiddenGrowth: string compatibility behavior outside explicit lossy conversion
entries.evidenceOwner: OS string nullable-string snapshots and native facade manifest guard

entries.whyNotLoweringToday: The helper exposes typed std::path::PathBuf construction, join/push, and lossy display methods until path value lowering covers the facade directly.
forbiddenGrowth: platform abstraction beyond explicit PathBuf methods
entries.evidenceOwner: path buffer snapshots and native facade manifest guard

entries.whyNotLoweringToday: The nullable-string variant mirrors path_buf_tools.rs while bridging to hxrt::string::HxString for the portable string contract.
forbiddenGrowth: string compatibility behavior outside explicit PathBuf methods
entries.evidenceOwner: path buffer nullable-string snapshots and native facade manifest guard

entries.whyNotLoweringToday: The helper keeps a crate-local name-distinct Rust String facade until native String methods lower directly.
forbiddenGrowth: allocation-heavy adapters beyond explicit facade methods
entries.evidenceOwner: rust.StringTools snapshots and native facade manifest guard

entries.whyNotLoweringToday: The nullable-string variant mirrors rust_string_tools.rs while accepting hxrt::string::HxString under the portable string contract.
forbiddenGrowth: portable StringTools compatibility beyond explicit facade methods
allocation-heavy adapters beyond explicit facade methods
entries.evidenceOwner: rust.StringTools nullable-string snapshots and native facade manifest guard

entries.whyNotLoweringToday: The helper exposes borrowed slice operations and the explicit toArray bridge until slice method lowering and owned Array conversion are represented directly.
entries.evidenceOwner: test/snapshot/rust_array_slice_views, borrow-region negative fixtures, and native facade manifest guard

entries.whyNotLoweringToday: The helper exposes typed Vec operations and explicit Array conversion bridges until Vec/Array lowering covers this facade directly.
entries.evidenceOwner: rust.VecTools snapshots, slice-view fixtures, and native facade manifest guard
